question.text: Under what circumstances can differential pricing be offered to retail individual investors in a book building IPO?

answer.text: Retail investors can be offered shares at any price determined by the issuer
Retail investors can receive a discount of up to 10% of the price at which shares are offered to institutional investors
Differential pricing is prohibited under SEBI ICDR Regulations for any category of investors
Retail investors must always pay a premium of 5% over the institutional price to ensure fair allotment

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI ICDR Regulations permit a discount of up to 10% of the institutional price for retail individual investors in a book building issue. This is a pro-retail measure to encourage retail participation. The discount must be disclosed in the offer document. **The Trap:** The cap is 10% -- not unlimited. Also, it is a discount (lower price), not a premium (higher price). Differential pricing is specifically allowed, not prohibited. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series II-A covers differential pricing as an investor protection measure. Series IX covers how BRLMs structure the pricing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always check if an IPO offers a retail discount. Even a 5-10% discount on a well-priced IPO significantly improves the retail investor's entry point. Factor this into your recommendation when comparing the IPO price to peer valuations.
